mnemonics.title: Green IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: The 4 R's of Green IT: Recycle, Reduce, Reuse, Renew - helps remember core sustainability practices

mnemonics.title: Energy Optimization Levels
mnemonics.content: HaSyApUs (Hardware → System → Application → User) - Remember: 'Happy Apps Use' - the hierarchy of energy optimization

mnemonics.title: Sleep States
mnemonics.content: S1 to S4: S1=Standby (light sleep), S2=Standby with CPU off, S3=Suspend to RAM (deep sleep), S4=Hibernate (deepest sleep) - ascending S means more savings

mnemonics.title: Data Center Metrics
mnemonics.content: PUE CUE WUE: Power, Carbon, Water Usage Effectiveness - remember as 'PCW: Pretty Clean Water' for green data centers

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency; lower is better (ideal = 1.0)
Measures carbon emissions per unit of IT energy; lower is better
Adjusts CPU power based on workload to save energy
Measures water consumption for cooling in data centers
Evaluates environmental impact from production to disposal
Ratio of renewable energy to total energy consumption
The broader field encompassing computing and telecommunications

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pie chart: If PUE=2.0, half the energy goes to IT (the useful slice), half to cooling/lighting. Goal: make the IT slice as large as possible (approach 1.0)
Think of a car throttle: Press lightly (low frequency) for highway cruising (saves fuel), press hard (high frequency) for overtaking (uses more fuel)
One big building (physical server) with many small apartments (virtual machines) - more efficient use of space and resources
Pyramid with Hardware at base (foundational), System above, Application next, User at top - each level builds on the previous

analogies.analogy: Like adjusting the speed of a fan based on room temperature - fast when hot, slow when cool, saves electricity
Like a multiplex theater showing multiple movies on one building instead of building separate single-screen theaters
Like sleeping vs. napping - S3 is like deep sleep (brain mostly off), S4 is like being in a coma (completely shut down)
Like carpooling - more people in fewer cars = less fuel consumed overall
Like keeping frequently used items on your desk instead of going to the storage room every time
